problems.title: Minimum No Of Platforms
problems.description: Implement Minimum No Of Platforms inside main() using the sample input shown below, then print the greedy algorithm result in the same format as the example. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Implement Minimum No Of Platforms inside main() using the sample input shown below, then print the greedy algorithm result in the same format as the example.
problems.solution:
def main():
    def findPlatform(arrivals, departures):
        n = len(arrivals)
        arrivals.sort()
        departures.sort()
        platform_needed = 0
        max_platforms = 0
        i = 0
        j = 0
        while i < n and j < n:
            if arrivals[i] < departures[j]:
                platform_needed += 1
                i += 1
                max_platforms = max(max_platforms, platform_needed)
            else:
                platform_needed -= 1
                j += 1
        return max_platforms

    arrivals = [900, 940, 950, 1100, 1500, 1800]

    departures = [910, 1200, 1120, 1130, 1900, 2000]

    print('Minimum Number of Platforms Required =', findPlatform(arrivals, departures))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

problems.title: Egyptian Fraction
problems.description: Implement Egyptian Fraction inside main() using the sample input shown below, then print the greedy algorithm result in the same format as the example. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Implement Egyptian Fraction inside main() using the sample input shown below, then print the greedy algorithm result in the same format as the example.
problems.solution:
def main():
    def egyptian_fraction(numerator, denominator):
        result = []
        while numerator != 0:
            unit_fraction_denom = (denominator + numerator - 1) // numerator
            result.append(unit_fraction_denom)
            numerator = numerator * unit_fraction_denom - denominator
            denominator *= unit_fraction_denom
        return result

    numerator = 5

    denominator = 6

    fractions = egyptian_fraction(numerator, denominator)

    print('Egyptian Fraction representation of {}/{}: '.format(numerator, denominator), end='')

    for denom in fractions:
        print(f'1/{denom}', end=' + ' if denom != fractions[-1] else '')

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
numerator = 5
denominator = 6
fractions = egyptian_fraction(numerator, denominator)
